timid_tmi.sotruebestie2: There could be something, but TMI could not pick it up at the moment.
timid_tmi.F1Please: Hold F1 to access other menu
timid_tmi.F1F2F3Please: Hold F1 to access other menu, F3 to cycle left, F4 to cycle right
timid_tmi.rapid_reform: Lagging wing members catch up more quickly
timid_tmi.leader_no_swarm: Leader will start a new attack run on reaching target
timid_tmi.wingmen_no_swarm: Wing members will keep their position in the wing on reaching target
timid_tmi.match_leader_facing: Wing members will not face towards target but will match wing leader
timid_tmi.attack_at_an_angle: Aim to pass near target instead of directly over it, based on weapon range
timid_tmi.independent_of_carrier: Combat won't end when only these are left on the field
timid_tmi.auto_fighter: Allows ships with Automated Ship to equip these wings.
timid_tmi.rd_no_extra_craft: Prevents Reserve Deployment from deploying extra wings when used
timid_tmi.rd_force_extra_craft: (DEFUNCT/OBSOLETE) Allows Reserve Deployment to grant extra wings regardless
timid_tmi.no_drop: Prevents from being salvaged or recovered
timid_tmi.ANTI_FTR: In conjunction with PD_ONLY, allows point defense weapons to target fighters in addition to missiles. On missiles, informs the AI to fire the weapon at fighters as well
timid_tmi.AUTOZOOM: Makes viewpoint automatically snap to target
timid_tmi.BOMB: Used on missiles. Force the weapon fire at least once every 3 seconds when target in range
timid_tmi.CONSERVE_1: Informs the AI to be hesitant in firing the last shot of a limited-ammo weapon. For weapons with a burst size larger than one, it counts full bursts and not individual shots
timid_tmi.CONSERVE_2: Informs the AI to be hesitant in firing the last two shots/bursts
timid_tmi.CONSERVE_3: Informs the AI to be hesitant in firing the last three shots/bursts
timid_tmi.CONSERVE_4: Informs the AI to be hesitant in firing the last four shots/bursts
timid_tmi.CONSERVE_5: Informs the AI to be hesitant in firing the last five shots/bursts
timid_tmi.CONSERVE_FOR_ANTI_ARMOR: Informs the AI to be hesitant in firing against stripped armor of a target and prefer firing at more armored section of a target like the Breach SRM.
timid_tmi.DIRECT_AIM: Informs the missile AI to not weave while approaching target
timid_tmi.DO_NOT_AIM: Used primarily for missile weapons, this will make the AI encourage to not aim and fire immediately as possible. Overrides conserve hints
timid_tmi.DO_NOT_CONSERVE: Informs the AI to be liberal in firing a limited-ammo weapon, firing at any opportunity
timid_tmi.EXTRA_RANGE_ON_FIGHTER: Informs the AI to fire the weapon at listed range plus 500 when mounted on fighters
timid_tmi.FIRE_WHEN_INEFFICIENT: Informs AI to fire the weapon even when not flux efficient
timid_tmi.GUIDED_POOR: Used for missiles. Informs the AI to fire at up to 10 degrees outside of arc
timid_tmi.HEATSEEKER: Used for missiles. Missile targets engines in a manner similar to the Salamander MRM
timid_tmi.MISSILE_SPREAD: Used for missiles. Makes missiles aim randomly off the target's center in a manner similar to the Breach SRM or Squall MLRS
timid_tmi.IGNORES_FLARES: Enable the weapon to identify decoy flares and not fire on them
timid_tmi.NO_TURN_RATE_BOOST_WHEN_IDLE: Prevents this weapon mounted to benefit from a 5x turn rate multiplier acquired on non-hardpoints when not firing.
timid_tmi.PD: Allows the weapon to target missiles. Weapon will still target ships but prefer missiles in range
timid_tmi.PD_ALSO: Used concurrently with PD. If used with PD, the weapon will only target missiles if other targets are not available
timid_tmi.PD_ONLY: Allows the weapon to only target missiles, unless paired with ANTI_FTR
timid_tmi.SHOW_IN_CODEX: Makes a SYSTEM weapon appear in the codex
timid_tmi.SYSTEM: For things like flares, built-in weapons and decorative weapons. Hides the weapon from the codex and prevents it from being dropped as loot
timid_tmi.STRIKE: This means that this weapon will only be used against ships destroyer-size or above. It will also grant any ship carrying this weapon the strike ability
timid_tmi.USE_VS_FRIGATES: Used concurrently with the STRIKE hint. If a weapon contains the STRIKE hint, this will make it so that weapon can be fired against frigates as well
timid_tmi.CIVILIAN: Changes AI behaviour to usually be more timid
timid_tmi.CARRIER: Changes AI behavior to primarily fight using its fighters
timid_tmi.COMBAT: Changes AI behavior to additionally fight using its weapons
timid_tmi.NO_AUTO_ESCORT: Changes AI behavior to not require escorts or automatically escort friendlies
timid_tmi.STATION: Becomes uncontrollable, start already deployed, and avoid moving
timid_tmi.HIDE_IN_CODEX: No entry for this ship in the codex, may not appear in dealer menu
timid_tmi.INDEPENDENT_ROTATION: Prevents this ship's facing from being locked.
timid_tmi.ALWAYS_PANIC: Makes ship always fire missile weapons as if close to death
timid_tmi.WEAPONS_FRONT_TO_BACK: Changes tiebreaker for weapon render order to be distance from front of ship
timid_tmi.WEAPONS_BACK_TO_FRONT: Changes tiebreaker for weapon render order to be distance from back of ship
timid_tmi.PHASE: Classify this as a phase ship (or not?)
timid_tmi.hist1t: First tier of historian's random blueprint searches
timid_tmi.hist2t: Second tier of historian's random blueprint searches
timid_tmi.hist3t: Third tier of historian's random blueprint searches
timid_tmi.rare_bp: Will appear as a standalone blueprint
timid_tmi.req_military: Will only show up in military markets
timid_tmi.auto_rec: Requires Automated Ships or something similar to recover
timid_tmi.no_dealer: May not appear in most dealer menu to be produced
timid_tmi.help1a: On your left, you see a list of factions.
timid_tmi.help1b: On your right, you see a list of related entries if the group exist for that faction.
timid_tmi.help1d: Use [%s] / [%s] to go up a level.
timid_tmi.help1e: The faction and its associated entries on the right can refresh when reloading your 'save.'
timid_tmi.winga: Wings take form of a LPC which can be slotted onto a fighter bay of a ship. Each wing has an engagement range which determines an approximate distance a wing can travel from the ship that launched it. Multiple wings launched from the same ship will form an attack run; trying to match their same speed to the slowest wing until their attack run range has been reached. Wings are split into 4 distinct roles.
timid_tmi.wingb: • Fighter has a generalist focus, serving no particular special role.
timid_tmi.wingc: • Interceptor focus on attacking enemy wings and distracting enemy wings.
timid_tmi.wingd: • Bomber focuses on destroying enemy ships with its specialty depending on its payload.
timid_tmi.winge: • Support focuses on keeping their parent carrier and friendly ships around alive.
timid_tmi.frigatea: Frigates are the smallest size of a ship that can work independently. Their role excels at capturing command points, harassing enemies, or drawing enemy firepower. Some may work as an excellent flagship in the hand of a capable pilot.
timid_tmi.destroyera: Destroyers, while not as fast or durable as other ships, are often a good choice for many fleets. They serve as good escorts for both cruisers and capital ships. Some may provide support fire, cover flanks, or specialize in frigate hunting.
timid_tmi.cruisera: Cruisers, with high peak performance time and versatility, are used as flagships in many fleets. They serve in the middle ideal mix of a capital ship's gunpowder and destroyer's speed. Some can utilize ships of these size to singlehandeded decide a battle.
timid_tmi.capitalshipa: Capital Ships, the biggest ships on the battlefield, are a formidable force on the battlefield. Often fitted with range hullmods allow them to deliver their immense firepower from afar. While incredibly difficult to destroy, their limited movement and positioning allowing flankers to utilize this disadvantage.
timid_tmi.prioritysorta: Each faction, including yours, can prioritize specific ships, weapons and fighters. Priority grants a strong likelihood is much more likely to spawn in the faction's fleets and markets, making it easier to obtain. Be it through fighting the faction, or buying it from their markets.
timid_tmi.hullmoda: Hullmods are modifications installed on the ship. They can, for example, affect how fast the ship is, improving performance of its shield, or adding more armor. Some hullmods, when built-in, provide additional bonuses on top of base bonus from the hullmod. Some hullmods, especially more expensive ones when built-in provide a negative effect in addition to the initial bonus.
timid_tmi.hullmodb: Each faction has a certain lineup of hullmods they can sell on their markets. More rare hullmods are often sold on military markets only, possibly restricted to those that have a commission with the faction. Hullmods can also be recovered as salvage.
timid_tmi.hullmodc: Less reputable spacers may resort to fighting fleets who already use the hullmods they want and hope to find them in salvage after the battle.
timid_tmi.hullmodd: Entries in this category may have hullmods be used in the fleet, but never sold on the market; hullmods sold on the market, but not used in the fleet; or neither.
timid_tmi.ballistica: Ballistic weapons can be slotted onto Ballistic, Hybrid, Composite, and Universal slot type of same size. Ballistic weapons can only be downsized onto only Ballistic slots.
timid_tmi.compositea: Composite weapons can be slotted onto Composite, Ballistic, Missile, and Universal slot type of same size. Composite weapons can only be downsized onto only Composite slots.
timid_tmi.missilea: Missile weapons can be slotted onto Missile, Composite, Synergy, and Universal slot type of same size. Missile weapons can only be downsized onto only Missile slots.
timid_tmi.synergya: Synergy weapons can be slotted onto Synergy, Energy, Missile, and Universal slot type of same size. Synergy weapons can only be downsized onto only Synergy slots.
timid_tmi.energya: Energy weapons can be slotted onto Energy, Hybrid, Synergy, and Universal slot type of same size. Energy weapons can only be downsized onto only Energy slots.
timid_tmi.hybrida: Hybrid weapons can be slotted onto Hybrid, Ballistic, Energy, and Universal slot type of same size. Hybrid weapons can only be downsized onto only Hybrid slots.
timid_tmi.universala: Universal weapons can be slotted onto any slot type of same size. Universal weapons can only be downsized onto only Universal slots.
